name: Gut nitric-oxide sensor (NsrR + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: An ingestible sensor for nitric oxide, a marker of intestinal inflammation. The native NsrR NO-sensing repressor gates a CRISPRi circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in the probiotic E. coli Nissle 1917.

input.detects: Luminal nitric oxide produced by host iNOS during gut inflammation

sensing.sensorMechanism: NsrR is a [2Fe-2S] repressor that releases its operator (e.g. the hmp promoter) when NO modifies its cluster.
sensing.signalFlow: NO -> NsrR releases PnsrR/Phmp -> transcribes an anti-reporter sgRNA -> CRISPRi inverts a constitutive reporter (NOT). Pair an inverter or memory switch for a persistent inflammation record.

parts.name: NsrR regulator
parts.notes: NO-responsive [2Fe-2S] repressor; endogenous to E. coli Nissle.

parts.name: sfGFP reporter
parts.notes: Recoverable from stool for non-invasive readout.

performance.notes: NsrR NO-sensing and CRISPRi are validated separately; integrated device is a design template. Complements the thiosulfate/tetrathionate/peroxide inflammation sensors.

safety.biocontainment: Probiotic E. coli Nissle host; add thyA/dapA auxotrophy for gut-restricted containment.
safety.notes: Probiotic chassis with a human-safety record; research / supervised clinical use only.

buildSteps.title: Design anti-reporter sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target the reporter promoter; check Nissle off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: Phmp -> sgRNA (native NsrR). TU2: dCas9 + constitutive sfGFP. Low-copy vector.

buildSteps.title: Validate in vitro
buildSteps.description: Confirm NO-donor response across a standard curve before any animal work.

buildSteps.title: Recover and measure
buildSteps.description: Recover cells from stool; quantify fluorescence.

provenance.source: Design template combining the native E. coli NsrR nitric-oxide sensor with a dCas9 CRISPRi circuit in E. coli Nissle
partsSources: Tucker et al. / Spiro / NsrR nitric-oxide sensing (E. coli)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
